race.name: Fjälltopploppet
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers across the Swedish mountain plateau where treeline gives way to open fjäll and Arctic wind.

vitals.aid_stations: 4 stations with warm drinks, energy bars, and chocolate

climate.description: The Swedish fjäll delivers harsh, exposed mountain conditions. Race day temperatures range from -15°C to -3°C, but wind chill on the open plateau can make it feel far colder. Above treeline, wind speeds of 40-60 km/h are not uncommon. Late March brings longer daylight but the mountain plateau remains deeply frozen.
challenges: Extended above-treeline sections with severe wind exposure
Whiteout conditions possible on the open fjäll plateau
Temperature drop of 5-10°C between forest and mountain sections
Late-season sun combined with high altitude increases UV risk

course.primary: Point-to-point mountain course that climbs from forested valleys to the open fjäll plateau above treeline. Extended exposed sections across the windswept mountain top before descending back to sheltered forest. One of Sweden's most dramatic Nordic race courses.
course.grooming: Groomed where possible; above-treeline sections rely on wind-packed and mechanically prepared snow
features: Extended above-treeline skiing on the open fjäll plateau
Dramatic transition from boreal forest to Arctic tundra landscape
Mountain-top panoramic views across the Härjedalen mountain range
One of the highest-altitude Nordic races in Scandinavia

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Sum 43/70 = 61.43, rounds to 61. The dramatic above-treeline mountain course and outstanding scenery push this into TIER 2 despite a small field.

history.summary: Founded in 1982, Fjälltopploppet was created to showcase the unique above-treeline skiing terrain of the Swedish fjäll. Bruksvallarna has been a training base for Swedish national ski teams for decades, and the race takes advantage of the extensive trail network and mountain infrastructure. The course's passage across the open fjäll makes it one of the most exposed and dramatic Nordic races in Scandinavia.
notable_facts: Bruksvallarna is a regular Swedish national team training venue
The course crosses the open fjäll plateau above 900 meters
The race has been shortened or rerouted in extreme wind years
Härjedalen is one of Sweden's least populated regions with vast wilderness
